title: COAR Notify: Technical lessons after 6 months of development
author: Walk, Paul
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The COAR Notify Initiative is developing and accelerating community adoption of a standard, interoperable, and decentralised approach to linking research outputs hosted in the distributed network of repositories with resources from external services such as overlay-journals and open peer review services, using W3C Linked Data Notifications. Several projects have started to implement the COAR Notify Protocol, with more scheduled to begin later this year. As these early adopters begin to implement the protocol to solve real-world problems, we are gaining valuable experience about good practice with asynchronous messaging using W3C Linked Data Notifications. This presentation will introduce the COAR Notify Protocol and then outline three areas where we are already establishing lessons, good practice and technical recommendations.
